name: I fell off a ladder at work yesterday, what do I do right now?
description: Seek medical care immediately, report to employer in writing today, photograph everything, and don't talk to insurance without a lawyer.

mainEntity.name: I fell off a ladder at work yesterday, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Seek medical care immediately, report to employer in writing today, photograph everything, and don't talk to insurance without a lawyer.
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Immediately seek medical attention** to document your injuries and notify your employer in writing within 24-48 hours.

Workers' compensation typically covers job-related ladder falls as they arise from employment hazards. Delaying reports can jeopardize claims, as most states require notice within 30 days.

- Get emergency care if needed; minor injuries still warrant a doctor's visit for records.
- Report the incident to your supervisor or HR promptly, detailing how the fall occurred.
- Avoid signing any statements without understanding them.

Take photos of the ladder, site, and injuries; save all medical records. Consult a workers' comp attorney before discussing with insurance adjusters. Utah and Idaho have strict reporting deadlines.[1][9]
